metadata.seed: Well, I'm working full time. I'm a part time student. I've just gotten engaged, and I live at home. There's just so much stress in my life. I have everything happening all at once. Just to top it all off, I failed an exam, a really big exam, and I just feel like I can't do anything anymore. I can't do anything right. I've just got so much stress, and I feel like such a disappointment, such a failure. And I've embarrassed my family by failing. I just don't know what to do anymore. I guess I've come here today to tell you, like, I want to leave uni, and I guess that's why I've come here to you today.

patient.query: Well, I'm working full time. I'm a part time student. I've just gotten engaged, and I live at home. There's just so much stress in my life. I have everything happening all at once. Just to top it all off, I failed an exam, a really big exam, and I just feel like I can't do anything anymore. I can't do anything right. I've just got so much stress, and I feel like such a disappointment, such a failure. And I've embarrassed my family by failing. I just don't know what to do anymore. I guess I've come here today to tell you, like, I want to leave uni, and I guess that's why I've come here to you today.

What evidence do you have to support these beliefs about yourself? And what might be some alternative ways of interpreting your situation?

patient.query: Honestly, it feels like there’s this heavy weight on my chest. I keep thinking about all the things I wanted to achieve and how I keep falling short. I guess, I just look at my past mistakes and it feels like they define me somehow. It's hard to see anything good when I’m stuck in that mindset. But, maybe there are moments where I’ve done okay... I just struggle to hold onto those.

automatic_thoughts: It's hard to see anything good

It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden of self-criticism, and it's understandable that this might make you feel anxious. Let's explore together if there are other ways to interpret your past experiences without defining yourself by your mistakes.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just really hard to let go of that negativity. I mean, every time I think I’m starting to feel better, something happens that drags me back down. It’s like I can’t escape this loop of feeling not good enough. Sometimes, I just wish I could see myself differently, but I don’t even know what that would look like.

triggers: loop of feeling not good enough
automatic_thoughts: I wish I could see myself differently

Sometimes I just wish I could turn off my brain for a bit.

patient.query: Thank you for saying that. It feels nice to know I can talk about this without being judged, but still, I find myself wanting to hide a lot of the time. I guess I fear that if I really open up, I’ll just end up feeling worse. I want to be honest about how I feel, but there’s this part of me that keeps holding back, like it’s too risky. It’s frustrating.

it's too risky to be honest

patient.query: I see why you'd think that, but it’s really hard for me to trust that it wouldn’t just make things messier. I mean, every time I’ve tried to be honest about my struggles in the past, it felt like people didn’t really get it, or they judged me. I guess I worry that if I let my guard down, maybe I’ll just feel more exposed and vulnerable. It’s like, can I really handle that?
